title: S-CAD: Selective Classical Advantage Distillation for Quantum Conference Key Agreement
authors: Trevor Thomas, Walter O. Krawec, Bing Wang
abstract: Quantum conference key agreement (QCKA) protocols utilize GHZ states to establish shared group keys between multiple parties. While previous work has shown that standard Classical Advantage Distillation (CAD) protocols can sometimes benefit QCKA performance, it was unknown if past results were asymptotically tight. In this work, we design a new CAD protocol, "Selective Classical Advantage Distillation (S-CAD)", for QCKA, which generalizes prior QCKA+CAD work and allows the parties to selectively enable or disable CAD. We derive an asymptotic proof of security against general coherent attacks, which outperforms prior work. Finally, we evaluate in a variety of simulated star network topologies, showing when S-CAD can help, and when it is best to disable CAD entirely.
summary: This paper presents a new protocol called S-CAD for Quantum Conference Key Agreement that allows users to selectively use classical advantage distillation. It provides a new security proof against coherent attacks that is more efficient than previous work and demonstrates through simulations when the protocol is most effective.
main_problem: Determining the optimal use of Classical Advantage Distillation (CAD) in Quantum Conference Key Agreement (QCKA) and establishing whether previous performance bounds were asymptotically tight.
main_result: The authors introduce S-CAD, a new protocol that allows for selective activation of CAD, and provide an asymptotic proof of security against general coherent attacks that outperforms previous methods.
method: The authors design a new protocol (S-CAD) that generalizes existing QCKA+CAD methods and use asymptotic security proofs and simulations of star network topologies to evaluate performance.
model_or_system: The protocol utilizes GHZ states to establish shared group keys among multiple parties in a star network topology.
key_observables: Secret key rate and security against coherent attacks.
important_parameters: The decision to enable or disable CAD and the network topology (star network).
main_assumptions: The security proof is derived against general coherent attacks.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the S-CAD protocol, provides an asymptotic security proof, and concludes with a numerical evaluation of various star network topologies.
